       spvPermutations[4]);\n  2033→                }\n  2034→                else\n  2035→                {\n  2036→                    SLANG_UNEXPECTED(\"Unsupported tensor dimension\");\n  2037→                }\n  2038→            }\n  2039→        case kIROp_MatrixType:\n  2040→            {\n  2041→                auto matrixType = static_cast<IRMatrixType*>(inst);\n  2042→                auto vectorSpvType = ensureVectorType(\n  2043→                    static_cast<IRBasicType*>(matrixType->getElementType())->getBaseType(),\n  2044→                    static_cast<IRIntLit*>(matrixType->getColumnCount())->getValue(),\n  2045→                    nullptr);\n  2046→                const auto columnCount =\n  2047→                    static_cast<IRIntLit*>(matrixType->getRowCount())->getValue();\n  2048→                auto matrixSPVType = emitOpTypeMatrix(\n  2049→                    inst,\n  2050→                    vectorSpvType,\n  2051→                    SpvLiteralInteger::from32(int32_t(columnCount)));\n  2052→                return matrixSPVType;\n  2053→            }\n  2054→        case kIROp_ArrayType:\n  2055→        case kIROp_UnsizedArrayType:\n  2056→            {\n  2057→                auto irArrayType = static_cast<IRArrayTypeBase*>(inst);\n  2058→                const auto elementType = irArrayType->getElementType();\n  2059→                const auto arrayType =\n  2060→                    inst->getOp() == kIROp_ArrayType\n  2061→                        ? emitOpTypeArray(inst, elementType, irArrayType->getElementCount())\n  2062→                        : emitOpTypeRuntimeArray(inst, elementType);\n  2063→                // Arrays of opaque types should not emit a stride\n  2064→                if (!isIROpaqueType(elementType) &&\n  2065→                    shouldEmitArrayStride(irArrayType->getElementType()))\n  2066→                {\n  2067→                    auto stride = 0;\n  2068→                    // If the array type has no stride, it indicates that this array type is only\n  2069→                    // used in Private or Function storage class (aka. thread-local or function\n  2070→                    // scope variable), in that case SPIRV doesn't allow to decorate the array\n  2071→                    // stride.\n  2072→                    //\n  2073→                    // The only exception is if the array type is unsized, because unsized array\n  2074→                    // also has no stride operand, however unsized array can not be used in Private\n  2075→                    // or Function, so we are safe to just decorate the stride for unsized array by\n  2076→                    // calculating the natural stride.\n  2077→                    if (auto strideInst = irArrayType->getArrayStride())\n  2078→                    {\n  2079→                        stride = (int)getIntVal(strideInst);\n  2080→                    }\n  2081→                    else if (inst->getOp() == kIROp_UnsizedArrayType)\n  2082→                    {\n  2083→                        IRSizeAndAlignment sizeAndAlignment;\n  2084→                        getNaturalSizeAndAlignment(\n  2085→                            m_targetProgram->getOptionSet(),\n  2086→                            elementType,\n  2087→                            &sizeAndAlignment);\n  2088→                        stride = (int)sizeAndAlignment.getStride();\n  2089→                    }\n  2090→\n  2091→                    if (stride != 0)\n  2092→                    {\n  2093→                        emitOpDecorateArrayStride(\n  2094→                            getSection(SpvLogicalSectionID::Annotations),\n  2095→                            nullptr,\n  2096→                            arrayType,\n  2097→                            SpvLiteralInteger::from32(stride));\n  2098→                    }\n  2099→                }\n  2100→                return arrayType;\n  2101→            }\n  2102→        case kIROp_AtomicType:\n  2103→            {\n  2104→                auto result = ensureInst(as<IRAtomicType>(inst)->getElementType());\n  2105→                registerInst(inst, result);\n  2106→                return result;\n  2107→            }\n  2108→        case kIROp_DescriptorHandleType:\n  2109→            {\n  2110→                IRBuilder builder(inst);\n  2111→                builder.setInsertBefore(inst);\n  2112→                return emitOpTypeVector(inst, builder.getUIntType(), SpvLiteralInteger::from32(2));\n  2113→            }\n  2114→        case kIROp_SubpassInputType:\n  2115→            return ensureSubpassInputType(inst, cast<IRSubpassInputType>(inst));\n  2116→        case kIROp_TextureType:\n  2117→            return ensureTextureType(inst, cast<IRTextureType>(inst));\n  2118→        case kIROp_SamplerStateType:\n  2119→        case kIROp_SamplerComparisonStateType:\n  2120→            return emitOpTypeSampler(inst);\n  2121→\n  2122→        case kIROp_RaytracingAccelerationStructureType:\n  2123→            requireSPIRVAnyCapability({SpvCapabilityRayTracingKHR, SpvCapabilityRayQueryKHR});\n  2124→            ensureAnyExtensionDeclaration(\n  2125→                {UnownedStringSlice(\"SPV_KHR_ray_tracing\"),\n  2126→                 UnownedStringSlice(\"SPV_KHR_ray_query\")});\n  2127→            return emitOpTypeAccelerationStructure(inst);\n  2128→\n  2129→        case kIROp_RayQueryType:\n  2130→ 2133→\n  2134→        case kIROp_HitObjectType:\n  2135→            ensureExtensionDeclaration(UnownedStringSlice(\"SPV_NV_shader_invocation_reorder\"));\n  2136→          }\n  2157→            // > OpTypeForwardPointer\n  2158→\n  2159→        case kIROp_Func:\n  2160→            // [3.32.6: Function Instructions]\n  2161→            //\n  2162→            //        case kIROp_GlobalVar:\n  2208→            return emitGlobalVar(as<IRGlobalVar>(inst));\n  2209→        case kIROp_SPIRVAsmOperandBuiltinVar:\n  2210→            return emitBuiltinVar(inst);\n 2256→                    debugSource->getFileName(),\n  2257→                    spvStrHead);\n  2258→\n  2259→                for (Index start = 65535; start < sourceStr.getLength(); start +=                {\n  2282→                    IRBuilder builder(inst);\n  2283→                    builder.setInsertBefore(inst);\n  2284→                    auto translationUnit =     }\n  2297→                return result;\n  2298→            }\n  2299→        case kIROp_DebugBuildIdentifier:\n  2300→            {\n  2301→ static SpvStorageClass getSpvStorageClass(IRPtrTypeBase* ptrType)\n  2453→    {\n  2454→        SpvStorageClass storageClass = SpvStorageClassFunction;\n  2455→        if (ptrType &&    }\n  2461→\n  2462→    // https://registry.khronos.org/vulkan/specs/1.3/html/chap37.html#VUID-StandaloneSpirv-DescriptorSet-06491\n  2463→    // Only UniformConstant, Uniform or StorageBuffer storage class are allowed to be decorated with\n  2464→    // descriptor set or binding.\n  2465→    static inline bool isBindingAllowed(SpvStorageClass storageClass)\n  2466→    {\n  2467→         return true;\n  2473→        default:\n  2474→            return false;\n  2475→        }\n  2476→    }\n  2477→\n  2478→    SpvCapability getImageFormatCapability(SpvImageFormat format)\n  2479→    {\n  2480→        switch (format)\n  2481→        {\n  2482→        case SpvImageFormatUnknown:\n  2483→        case SpvImageFormatRgba32f:\n  2484→        case SpvImageFormatRgba16f:\n }\n  2504→\n  2505→    void setImageFormatCapabilityAndExtension(\n  2506→        SpvImageFormat format,\n  2507→        SpvCapability_ setCapabilityMask)\n  2508→    {\n  2509→        switch (format)\n  2510→        {\n  2511→        case SpvImageFormatUnknown:\n  2512→        case SpvImageFormatRgba32f:\n  2513→        case SpvImageFormatRgba16f:\n  2514→        case 2519→        case SpvImageFormatRgba8i:\n  2520→        case SpvImageFormatR32i:\n  2521→        case SpvImageFormatRgba32ui:\n  2522→        case SpvImageFormatRgba16ui:\n  2523→        case requireSPIRVCapability(SpvCapabilityInt64ImageEXT);\n  2535→            return;\n  2536→        default:\n  2537→            if (setCapabilityMask == SpvCapabilityStorageImageExtendedFormats)\n  2538→                return;\n  2539→            requireSPIRVCapability(SpvCapabilityStorageImageExtendedFormats);\n  2540→            return;\n  2541→        }\n  2542→    }\n  2543→\n  2544→    struct ImageOpConstants\n  2545→    {\n  2546→        enum : SpvWord\n  2547→        {\n  2548→            // Some untyped constants from OpTypeImage\n  2549→            // https://registry.khronos.org/SPIR-V/specs/unified1/SPIRV.html#OpTypeImage\n  2550→\n  2551→            // indicates not a depth image\n  2552→            notDepthImage = 0,\n  2553→            // indicates a depth image\n  2554→            isDepthImage = 1,\n  2555→            // means no indication as to whether this is a depth or non-depth image\n  2556→            unknownDepthImage = 2,\n  2557→\n  2558→            // indicates non-arrayed content\n  2559→            notArrayed = 0,\n  2560→            // indicates arrayed content\n  2561→            isArrayed = 1,\n  2562→\n  2563→            // indicates this is only known at run time, not at compile time\n  2564→            sampledUnknown = 0,\n  2565→            // indicates an image compatible with sampling operations\n  2566→            sampledImage = 1,\n  2567→            // indicates an image compatible with read/write operations (a storage or subpass data\n  2568→            // image).\n  2569→            readWriteImage = 2,\n  2570→\n  2571→            // indicates single-sampled content\n  2572→            notMultisampled = 0,\n  2573→            // indicates multisampled content\n  2574→            isMultisampled = 1,\n  2575→        };\n  2576→    };\n  2577→\n  2578→    SpvInst* ensureSubpassInputType(IRInst* assignee, IRSubpassInputType* inst)\n  2579→    {\n  2580→        IRInst* sampledType = inst->getElementType();\n  2581→        SpvDim dim = SpvDimSubpassData;\n  2582→        SpvWord ms = inst->isMultisample() ? ImageOpConstants::isMultisampled\n  2583→                                           : ImageOpConstants::notMultisampled;\n  2584→        SpvWord sampled = 2;\n  2585→        requireSPIRVCapability(SpvCapabilityInputAttachment);\n  2586→        requireSPIRVCapability(SpvCapabilityStorageImageReadWithoutFormat);\n  2587→        setImageFormatCapabilityAndExtension(SpvImageFormatUnknown, SpvCapabilityShader);\n  2588→        return emitOpTypeImage(\n  2589→            assignee,\n  2590→            getVectorElementType((IRType*)sampledType),\n  2591→            dim,\n  2592→            SpvLiteralInteger::from32(ImageOpConstants::unknownDepthImage),\n  2593→            SpvLiteralInteger::from32(0),\n  2594→            SpvLiteralInteger::from32(ms),\n  2595→            SpvLiteralInteger::from32(sampled),\n  2596→            SpvImageFormatUnknown);\n  2597→    }\n  2598→\n  2599→    SpvInst* ensureTextureType(IRInst* assignee, IRTextureTypeBase* inst)\n  2600→    {\n  2601→        IRInst* sampledType = inst->getElementType();\n  2602→        SpvDim dim = SpvDim1D; // Silence uninitialized warnings from msvc...\n  2603→        switch (inst->GetBaseShape())\n  2604→        {\n  2605→        case SLANG_TEXTURE_1D:\n  2606→            dim = SpvDim1D;\n  2607→            break;\n  2608→        case SLANG_TEXTURE_2D:\n  2609→            dim = SpvDim2D;\n  2610→            break;\n  2611→        case SLANG_TEXTURE_3D:\n  2612→            dim = SpvDim3D;\n  2613→            break;\n  2614→        case SLANG_TEXTURE_CUBE:\n  2615→            dim = SpvDimCube;\n  2616→            break;\n  2617→        case SLANG_TEXTURE_BUFFER:\n  2618→            dim = SpvDimBuffer;\n  2619→            break;\n  2620→        }\n  2621→        SpvWord arrayed =\n  2622→            inst->isArray() ? 2631→        switch (inst->getAccess())\n  2632→        {\n  2633→        case SlangResourceAccess::SLANG_RESOURCE_ACCESS_READ_WRITE:\n  2634→        case SlangResourceAccess::SLANG_RESOURCE_ACCESS_RASTER_ORDERED:\n  2635→        case SlangResourceAccess::SLANG_RESOURCE_ACCESS_WRITE:\n  2636→            sampled = ImageOpConstants::readWriteImage;\n  2637→            break;\n  2638→        case SlangResourceAccess::SLANG_RESOURCE_ACCESS_NONE:\n  2639→        case SlangResourceAccess::SLANG_RESOURCE_ACCESS_READ:\n  2640→            sampled =          sampledType = unwrapAttributedType(sampledType);\n  2650→            if (format == SpvImageFormatUnknown)\n  2651→            {\n  2652→                IRIntegerValue vectorSize = 1;\n  2653→                if (auto vecType = as<IRVectorType>(sampledType))\n  2654→                    vectorSize = getIntVal(vecType->getElementCount());\n  2655→\n  2656→                for (auto attr : attribType->getAllAttrs())\n  2657→                {\n  2658→                    switch (attr->getOp())\n  2659→                    {\n  2660→                    case kIROp_UNormAttr:\n  2661→                        switch (vectorSize)\n  2662→                        {\n  2663→                        case 1:\n  2664→                            format = SpvImageFormatR8;\n  2665→                            break;\n  2666→                        case 2:\n  2667→                            format = SpvImageFormatRg8;\n  2668→                            break;\n  2669→                        case 3:\n  2670→                            format = SpvImageFormatRgba8;\n  2671→                            break;\n  2672→                        case 4:\n  2673→                            format = SpvImageFormatRgba8;\n  2674→                            break;\n  2675→                        }\n  2676→                        break;\n  2677→                    case kIROp_SNormAttr:\n  2678→       }\n  2696→            }\n  2697→        }\n  2698→\n  2699→        //\n  2700→        // Capabilities, according to section 3.8\n  2701→        //\n  2702→        // SPIR-V requires that the sampled/rw info on the image isn't unknown\n  2703→        SLANG_ASSERT(\n  2704→            sampled == ImageOpConstants::sampledImage ||\n  2705→            sampled == ImageOpConstants::readWriteImage);\n  2706→        if (ms == ImageOpConstants::isMultisampled)\n  2707→            requireSPIRVCapability(SpvCapabilityStorageImageMultisample);\n  2708→        switch (dim)\n  2709→        {\n  2710→        case SpvDim1D:\n  2711→            requireSPIRVCapability(\n  2712→                sampled == ImageOpConstants::sampledImage ? SpvCapabilitySampled1D\n  2713→                                                          : SpvCapabilityImage1D);\n  2714→            break;\n  2715→        case SpvDim2D:\n  2716→            // Also requires Shader or Kernel, but these are a given (?)\n  2717→            if (sampled == ImageOpConstants::readWriteImage &&\n  2718→                ms == ImageOpConstants::isMultisampled && arrayed == ImageOpConstants::isArrayed)\n  2719→                requireSPIRVCapability(SpvCapabilityImageMSArray);\n  2720→            break;\n  2721→        case SpvDim3D:\n  2722→            break;\n  2723→        case SpvDimCube:\n  2724→            // Requires shader also\n  2725→            if (sampled == ImageOpConstants::readWriteImage &&\n  2726→                arrayed == ImageOpConstants::isArrayed)\n  2727→                requireSPIRVCapability(SpvCapabilityImageCubeArray);\n  2728→            break;\n  2729→        case SpvDimRect:\n  2730→            requireSPIRVCapability(\n  2731→                sampled == ImageOpConstants::sampledImage ? 2740→            SLANG_UNIMPLEMENTED_X(\"OpTypeImage Capabilities for SpvDimTileImageDataEXT\");\n  2741→            break;\n  2742→        }\n  2743→        if (format == SpvImageFormatUnknown && 2747→            requireSPIRVCapability(SpvCapabilityStorageImageReadWithoutFormat);\n  2748→            requireSPIRVCapability(SpvCapabilityStorageImageWriteWithoutFormat);\n  2749→        }\n  2750→\n  2751→        setImageFormatCapabilityAndExtension(format, SpvCapabilityShader);\n  2752→\n  2753→        //\n  2754→        // The op itself\n  2755→        //\n  2756→        auto nullptr,\n  2761→                sampledElementType,\n  2762→                dim,\n  2763→                SpvLiteralInteger::from32(depth),\n  2764→                SpvLiteralInteger::from32(arrayed),\n  2765→                SpvLiteralInteger::from32(ms),\n  2766→                SpvLiteralInteger::from32(sampled),\n  2767→                format);\n  2768→            dim,\n  2775→            SpvLiteralInteger::from32(depth),\n  2776→            SpvLiteralInteger::from32(arrayed),\n  2777→            SpvLiteralInteger::from32(ms),\n  2778→            SpvLiteralInteger::from32(sampled),\n  2779→            format);\n  2780→    }\n  2781→\n  2782→    // Ensures an SpvInst for the specified vector type is emitted.\n  2783→    // `inst` represents an 2823→    bool _maybeEmitInterpolationModifierDecoration(IRInterpolationMode mode, SpvId varInst)\n  2824→    {\n  2825→        switch (mode)\n  2826→        {\n  2827→        case void emitSystemVarDecoration(IRInst* var, SpvInst* varInst)\n  2862→    {\n  2863→        for (auto decor : var->getDecorations())\n  2864→        {\n  2865→            switch (decor->getOp())\n  2876→    }\n  2877→\n  2878→    void emitVarLayout(IRInst* var, SpvInst* varInst, IRVarLayout* layout)\n  2879→    {\n  2880→        auto dataType = as<IRPtrTypeBase>(var->getDataType());\n  2881→ the user didn't explicitly qualify a varying\n  3003→        // with integer type, then we need to explicitly\n  3004→        // add the `flat` modifier for GLSL.\n  3005→        if (!anyModifiers)\n  3054→    /// Emit a specialization constant.\n  3055→    SpvInst* emitSpecializationConstant(IRGlobalParam* param, IRVarOffsetAttr* offset)\n  3056→    {\n  3057→        IRInst* defaultVal =  }\n  3062→        else\n  3063→        {\n  3064→            IRBuilder builder(param);\n  3065→            builder.setInsertBefore(param);\n  3066→            defaultVal = 3081→                    param,\n  3082→                    param->getDataType());\n  3083→            }\n  3084→            else\n  3085→            {\n  3086→                result =           }\n  3091→        }\n  3092→        else if (auto type = as<IRBasicType>(defaultVal->getDataType()))\n  3093→        {\n  3094→            result = emitOpSpecConstant(\n  3095→     }\n  3100→        else if (as<IRVectorType>(defaultVal->getDataType()))\n  3101→        {\n  3102→            List<IRInst*> operands;\n  3103→            auto makeVector = 3107→            }\n  3108→            result = emitOpSpecConstantComposite(\n  3109→                getSection(SpvLogicalSectionID::ConstantsAndTypes),\n  3110→                param,\n  3111→                param->getDataType(),\n  3112→                operands);\n  3113→        }\n  3114→\n  3115→        emitOpDecorateSpecId(\n  3116→            getSection(SpvLogicalSectionID::Annotations),\n  3117→            nullptr,\n  3118→            result,\n  3119→            SpvLiteralInteger::from32((uint32_t)offset->getOffset()));\n  3120→\n  3121→        maybeEmitName(result, param);\n  3122→        return result;\n  3123→    }\n  3124→\n  3125→    SpvInst* emitSpecializationConstantOp(IRInst* inst)\n  3126→    {\n  3127→        SpvInst* spv = nullptr;\n  3128→        if (m_mapIRInstToSpvInst.tryGetValue(inst, spv))\n  3129→            return spv;\n  3130→\n  3131→        // For each OpSpecConstantOp, the operand must be:\n  3132→        // 1. Another OpSpecConstantOp\n  3135→\n  3136→        // For 1 and 2, we can just emit the specialization constant or literal constant.\n  3137→        if (auto param = as<IRGlobalParam>(inst))\n  3138→        {\n  3139→            auto layout = getVarLayout(param);\n  3140→            if (layout)\n  3141→            {\n  3142→                if (auto offset =\n  3143→                        layout->findOffsetAttr(LayoutResourceKind::SpecializationConstant))\n  3144→                {\n  3145→                    return emitSpecializationConstant(param, offset);\n  3146→                }\n  3147→            }\n  3148→            SLANG_UNREACHABLE(\"Non specialization constant used in OpSpecConstantOp\\n\");\n  3149→        }\n  3150→        else if (as<IRConstant>(inst))\n  3151→        {\n  3152→            // We need to emit the constant as a specialization constant\n  3153→            return emitLit(inst);\n  3154→        }\n  3155→\n  3156→        IRType* type = 43→\n    44→// [2.3: Physical Layout of a SPIR-V Module and Instruction]\n    45→//\n    46→// > A SPIR-V module is a single linear stream of words.\n    47→\n    48→\n    49→// [2.3: Physical Layout of a SPIR-V Module and Instruction]\n    50→//\n    51→// > All remaining words are a linear sequence of instructions.\n    52→// > Each instruction is a stream of words\n    53→//\n    54→// After a try to emit instructions directly\n    59→// in one pass as a stream of words, but there are additional\n    60→// constraints placed by the SPIR-V encoding that would make\n    61→// a single-pass strategy very hard, so we don't attempt it.\n    62→//\n    63→// [2.4 Logical Layout of a Module]\n    64→//\n    65→// SPIR-V imposes some global ordering constraints on instructions,\n    66→// such that certain instructions must come before or after others.\n    67→// For example, all `OpCapability` instructions must come before any\n    68→// `OpEntryPoint` instructions.\n    69→//\n    70→// While the SPIR-V spec doesn't use such a term, we will take\n    71→// the enumeration of the ordering in Section 2.4 and use it to\n    72→// define a list of *logical sections* that make up a ExtIntInstImports,\n    80→    MemoryModel,\n    81→    EntryPoints,\n    82→    ExecutionModes,\n    83→    DebugStringsAndSource,\n    84→    DebugNames,\n    85→    Annotations,\n    86→ SpvOpSRem : SpvOpUMod;\n   833→            break;\n   834→        case kIROp_FRem:\n   835→            opCode = SpvOpFRem;\n   836→            break;\n   837→        case kIROp_Less:\n   838→            opCode = isFloatingPoint ? SpvOpFOrdLessThan\n   839→                     : isSigned      ? SpvOpSLessThan\n   840→                                     : SpvOpULessThan;\n   841→            break;\n   842→        case kIROp_Leq:\n   843→            opCode = isFloatingPoint ? SpvOpFOrdLessThanEqual\n   844→                     : isSigned      ? SpvOpSLessThanEqual\n   845→                                     : SpvOpULessThanEqual;\n   846→            break;\n   847→        case kIROp_Eql:\n   848→            opCode = isFloatingPoint ? SpvOpFOrdEqual : isBool ? SpvOpLogicalEqual : SpvOpIEqual;\n   849→            break;\n   850→        case kIROp_Neq:\n   851→            opCode = isFloatingPoint ? SpvOpFUnordNotEqual\n   852→                     : isBool        ? SpvOpLogicalNotEqual\n   853→                                     : SpvOpINotEqual;\n   854→            break;\n   855→        case kIROp_Geq:\n   856→            opCode = isFloatingPoint ? SpvOpFOrdGreaterThanEqual\n   857→                     : isSigned      ? SpvOpSGreaterThanEqual\n   858→                                     : SpvOpUGreaterThanEqual;\n   859→            break;\n   860→        case kIROp_Greater:\n   861→            opCode = isFloatingPoint ? SpvOpFOrdGreaterThan\n   862→                     : isSigned      ? SpvOpSGreaterThan\n   863→                                     : SpvOpUGreaterThan;\n   864→            break;\n   865→        case kIROp_Neg:\n   866→            opCode = isFloatingPoint ? SpvOpFNegate : SpvOpSNegate;\n   867→            break;\n   868→        case kIROp_And:\n   869→            opCode = SpvOpLogicalAnd;\n   870→            break;\n   871→        case kIROp_Or:\n   872→            opCode = SpvOpLogicalOr;\n   873→            break;\n   874→        case kIROp_Not:\n   875→            opCode = SpvOpLogicalNot;\n   876→            break;\n   877→        case kIROp_BitAnd:\n   878→            if (isBool)\n   879→                opCode = SpvOpLogicalAnd;\n   880→            else\n   881→                opCode = SpvOpBitwiseAnd;\n   882→            break;\n   883→        case kIROp_BitOr:\n   884→            if (isBool)\n   885→                opCode = SpvOpLogicalOr;\n   886→            else\n   887→                opCode = SpvOpBitwiseOr;\n   888→            break;\n   889→        case kIROp_BitXor:\n   890→            if (isBool)\n   891→                opCode = SpvOpLogicalNotEqual;\n   892→            else\n   893→                opCode = SpvOpBitwiseXor;\n   894→            break;\n   895→        case kIROp_BitNot:\n   896→            if (isBool)\n   897→                opCode = SpvOpLogicalNot;\n   898→            else\n   899→                opCode = SpvOpNot;\n   900→            break;\n   901→        case kIROp_Rsh:\n   902→            opCode = isSigned ? SpvOpShiftRightArithmetic : SpvOpShiftRightLogical;\n   903→            break;\n   904→        case kIROp_Lsh:\n   905→            opCode = SpvOpShiftLeftLogical;\n   906→            break;\n   907→        }\n   908→        return opCode;\n   909→    }\n   910→    /// Ensure that an instruction has been emitted\n   911→    SpvInst* ensureInst(IRInst* irInst)\n   912→    {\n   913→        SpvInst* spvInst = nullptr;\n   914→        if (!m_mapIRInstToSpvInst.tryGetValue(irInst, spvInst))\n   915→        {\n   916→            // If the `irInst` hasn't already been emitted,\n   917→            // then we will assume that is is a global instruction\n   918→            // (a constant, type, function, etc.) and we should make\n   919→            // sure it gets emitted now.\n   920→            //\n   921→            // Note: this step means that emitting an instruction\n   922→            // can be re-entrant/recursive. Because we emit the SPIR-V\n   923→            // words for an instruction into an intermediate structure\n   924→            // we don't have to worry about the re-entrancy causing\n   925→            // the ordering of instruction words to be interleaved.\n   926→            //\n   927→            spvInst = emitGlobalInst(irInst);\n   928→        }\n   929→        return spvInst;\n   930→    }\n   931→\n   932→    // Whilst an instruction has been created, we append the operand\n   933→    // words to it with `emitOperand`. There are a few different\n   934→    // case of operands that we handle.\n   935→    //\n   936→    // The simplest case is when an instruction takes an operand\n   937→    // that is just a literal SPIR-V word.\n   938→\n   939→    /// Emit a literal `word` as an operand to the current instruction\n   940→    void emitOperand(SpvWord word)\n   941→    {\n   942→        // Can only add operands if we are constructing an instruction (ie in _beginInst/_endInst)\n   943→        SLANG_ASSERT(m_currentInst || m_peekingOperands);\n   944→        m_operandStack.add(word);\n   945→    }\n   946→\n   947→    // The most common case of operand is an <id> that represents\n   948→    // some other instruction. In cases where we already have\n   949→    // an <id> we can emit it as a literal and the meaning is\n   950→    // the same. If we have a `SpvInst` we can look up or\n   951→    // generate an <id> for it.\n   952→\n   953→    /// Emit an operand to the current instruction, which references `src` by its <id>\n   954→    void emitOperand(SpvInst* src) { emitOperand(getID(src)); }\n   955→\n   956→    // Commonly, we will have an operand in the form of an `IRInst`\n   957→    // which might either represent an instruction we've already\n   958→    // emitted (e.g., because it came earlier in a function body)\n   959→    // or which we have yet to emit (because it is a global-scope\n   960→    // instruction that has not been referenced before).\n   961→\n   962→    /// Emit an operand to the current instruction, which references `src` by its <id>\n   963→    void emitOperand(IRInst* src)\n   964→    {\n   965→        SpvInst* spvSrc = ensureInst(src);\n   966→        emitOperand(getID(spvSrc));\n   967→    }\n   968→\n   969→    // Some instructions take a string as a literal operand,\n   970→    // which requires us to follow the SPIR-V rules to\n   971→    // encode the string into multiple operand words.\n   972→\n   973→    /// Emit an operand that is encoded as a literal string\n   974→    void emitOperand(UnownedStringSlice const& text)\n   975→    {\n   976→        // Can only emitOperands if we are in an instruction\n   977→        SLANG_ASSERT(m_currentInst || m_peekingOperands);\n   978→        SLANG_COMPILE_TIME_ASSERT(sizeof(SpvWord) == 4);\n   979→\n   980→        // Assert that `text` doesn't contain any embedded nul bytes, since they\n   981→        // could lead to invalid encoded results.\n   982→        SLANG_ASSERT(text.indexOf(0) < 0);\n   983→        emitOperand(SpvLiteralBits::fromUnownedStringSlice(text));\n   984→    }\n   985→\n   986→    // Sometimes we will want to pass down an argument that\n   987→    // represents a result <id> operand, but we won't yet\n   988→    // have access to the `SpvInst` that will get the <id>.\n   989→    // We will use a dummy `enum` type to support this case.\n   990→\n   991→    enum ResultIDToken\n   992→    {\n   993→        kResultID\n   994→    };\n   995→\n   996→    void emitOperand(ResultIDToken)\n   997→ 465→    /// The Slang IR module being translated\n   466→    IRModule* m_irModule;\n   467→\n   468→    // [2.2: Terms]\n   469→    //\n   470→    // > <id>: A numerical name; the name used to refer to an object, a type,\n   471→    // > a function, a label, etc. An <id> always consumes one word.\n   472→    // > The <id>s defined by a module obey SSA.\n   473→    //\n   474→    // [2.3: Physical Layout of a SPIR-V Module and Instruction]\n   475→    //\n   476→    // > Bound; where all <id>s in this module are guaranteed to satisfy\n   477→    // > 0 < id < Bound\n   478→    // > Bound should be small, smaller is better, with all <id> in a module being densely packed\n   479→    // and near 0.\n   480→    //\n   481→    // Instructions will be referred to by their <id>s.\n   482→    // We need to generate <id>s for instructions, and also\n   483→    // compute the \"bound\" value that will be stored in\n   484→    // the module header.\n   485→    //\n   486→    // We will use a single counter and allocate <id>s\n   487→    // on demand. There may be some slop where we allocate\n   488→    // an <id> for something that never gets referenced,\n   489→    // but we expect the amount of slop to be small (and\n   490→    // it can be cleaned up by other tools/passes).\n   491→\n   492→    /// The next destination `<id>` to allocate.\n   493→    SpvWord m_nextID = 1;\n   494→\n   495→    OrderedDictionary<SpvInst*, IRPtrTypeBase*> m_forwardDeclaredPointers;\n   496→\n   497→    SpvInst* m_nullDwarfExpr = nullptr;\n   498→\n   499→    // A hash set to prevent redecorating the same spv inst.\n   500→    HashSet<SpvId> m_decoratedSpvInsts;\n   501→\n   502→    SpvAddressingModel m_addressingMode = SpvAddressingModelLogical;\n   503→\n   504→    // We will store the logical sections of the SPIR-V module\n   505→    // in a single array so that we can easily look up a\n   506→    // section by its `SpvLogicalSectionID`.\n   507→\n   508→    /// The logical sections of the SPIR-V module\n   509→    SpvLogicalSection m_sections[int(SpvLogicalSectionID::Count)];\n   510→\n   511→    /// Get a logical section based on its `SpvLogicalSectionID`\n   512→    SpvLogicalSection* getSection(SpvLogicalSectionID id) { return &m_sections[int(id)]; }\n   513→\n   514→    // At the end of emission we need a single linear stream of words,\n   515→    // so we will eventually flatten `m_sections` into a single array.\n   516→\n   517→    /// The final array of SPIR-V words that defines the encoded module\n   518→    List<SpvWord> m_words;\n   519→\n   520→    /// Emit the concrete words that make up the binary SPIR-V module.\n   521→    ///\n   522→    /// This function fills in `m_words` based on the data in `m_sections`.\n   523→    /// This function should only be called once.\n   524→    ///\n   525→    void emitPhysicalLayout()\n   526→    {\n   527→        // [2.3: Physical Layout of a SPIR-V Module and Instruction]\n   528→        //\n   529→        // > Magic Number\n   530→        //\n   531→        m_words.add(SpvMagicNumber);\n   532→\n   533→        // > Version nuumber\n   534→        //\n   535→        m_words.add(m_spvVersion);\n   536→\n   537→        // > Generator's magic number.\n   538→        //\n   539→        m_words.add(kSPIRVSlangCompilerId);\n   540→\n   541→        // > Bound\n   542→        //\n   543→        // As described above, we use `m_nextID` to allocate\n   544→        // <id>s, so its value when we are done emitting code\n   545→        // can serve as the bound.\n   546→        //\n   547→        m_words.add(m_nextID);\n   548→\n   549→        // > 0 (Reserved for instruction schema, if needed.)\n   550→        //\n   551→        m_words.add(0);\n   552→\n   553→        // > First word of instruction stream\n   554→        // > All remaining words are a linear sequence of instructions.\n   555→        //\n   556→        // Once we are done emitting the header, we emit all\n   557→        // the instructions in our logical sections.\n   558→        //\n   559→        for (int ii = 0;
